steps.title: Fit the shown line from two grid points
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+19$. Use Desmos as a quick table check: the line passes through $(-1,7)$ and $(0,3)$. A linear regression on those two points gives slope $-4$ and intercept $3$, so the shown line is $y=-4x+3$.

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content: Because the shown graph is $y=f(x)+19$, subtract 19 from the fitted equation: $$f(x)+19=-4x+3$$ $$f(x)=-4x-16$$

steps.title: Match the signs
steps.content: The function $f(x)=-4x-16$ has a negative slope and a negative y-intercept. With $c>0$ and $d>0$, the form $-d-cx$ also has a negative y-intercept and a negative slope.

steps.title: Match the form
steps.content: Choice A, $f(x)=-d-cx$, works with $d=16$ and $c=4$. The other choices have a positive intercept, a positive slope, or both. Choice A is correct.
